data.title: North American Construction Group CEO Joe Lambert Resigns
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

North American Construction Group said that Chief Executive Joe Lambert has resigned to pursue other opportunities.

The Canadian heavy equipment and mining services contractor said Wednesday that it has begun the process of assessing both internal and external candidates to assume the role on a permanent basis.

North American Construction said that Chief Operating Officer Barry Palmer has assumed the role of CEO on an interim basis, effective immediately.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Part found near Spain train crash site may be missing undercarriage, experts say
data.description:
Key points:
  • Photographed part lies outside cordoned investigation area
  • Iryo, Adif, and Transport Ministry silent on part's identity
  • Investigators had been searching for missing bogie

By Susana Vera and David Latona

A large piece of metal found near the site of a high-speed train crash in Spain may be the missing chunk of undercarriage that investigators have been searching for to find the cause of the accident, a source and experts said on Wednesday.

The crash, one of Europe's biggest, happened on Sunday night near the southern town of Adamuz in a remote hilly area, killing at least 42 people.

Inaki Barron, the head of Spain's railway accident investigating body CIAF, said on Monday that the investigation would focus on a missing part, known as a bogie, due to its importance as the key point of contact between the speeding train and the tracks and that it could shed light on the cause of the derailment.

Reuters photographs from Tuesday showed the piece lying partly submerged in a small stream to the side of a railway bridge some 15 metres (49 ft) below the train tracks and some 300 m from the crash site.

A spokesperson for the Civil Guard police force told Reuters by phone from Adamuz that the piece had been spotted by search teams using drones earlier in the week and had now been physically examined by investigators, adding: "We will look at it to determine what it is."

"Due to its size and weight, it has been left in the place it was found and has not yet been collected."

BOGIE FLEW OUT LIKE BULLET, SOURCE SAYS

A source briefed on the probe said the component was believed to be a so-called bogie - the train's wheeled undercarriage - that belonged to the first train that derailed, operated by private consortium Iryo.

Given the very high speed at which the two trains were travelling and the impact of the two trains, the bogie - though very heavy - flew out "like a bullet", the source, who declined to be named due to the sensitivity of the matter, said.

Investigators have combed the area around the crash site over the past three days, cordoning it off, marking and photographing every piece of evidence on the track. This piece had no markings and it was not within the cordoned area when it was photographed by Reuters on Tuesday afternoon.

"(The part) is currently under investigation by railway accident investigating body CIAF and we cannot add any information or comments outside the scope of that investigation," state rail company Renfe said in an emailed statement to Reuters on Wednesday, the day after the piece was photographed.

The Spanish Transport Ministry, Iryo and Adif did not immediately reply to requests for confirmation that the part photographed by Reuters was the missing bogie. Italy's Ferrovie dello Stato, the majority owner of Iryo, declined to comment due to the ongoing investigation.

EXPERTS SAY PHOTOGRAPH APPEARS TO BE A BOGIE

"Bogies - the element holding the wheels and suspension to the train - are often thrown loose during high-energy derailments," Scottish railway engineer and writer Gareth Dennis said.

"The location of this bogie and the section of the train it departed from will help investigators establish the precise trajectories of the derailed vehicles and the energies involved," Dennis added.

data.title: Belgian Bank Issues Debut European Green Bond Amid Geopolitical Tensions — Market Talk
data.description: Belgium's Argenta Spaarbank issued 500 million euros in a debut European green bond on Wednesday, LSEG data showed. The green bond is a senior non-preferred bond, a risky type of bank debt issued to meet regulatory capital requirements. The bond's maturity date is on Feb. 2, 2034. "The senior non-preferred bond is issued at a moment that the market's nervousness around Trump's Greenland related tariff intensions has fully cancelled out the positive start of the year, particularly for bail-in senior and AT1," ING's Maureen Schuller says in a note. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Brokerages cut Bombardier's rating on macro uncertainty, raise PTs
data.description:
** Brokerages Scotiabank and CIBC downgrade Canadian planemaker Bombardier's ratings; BMO maintains

** However, all raise price targets on co's upside potential from defense business and long-term growth

** Scotiabank flags decline in corporate profits or customer net worth, increase in pre-owned inventory, superior products by competitors and key executive turnover

** Adds geopolitical conflicts, global trade and macro uncertainty also pose additional risk

** Meanwhile, CIBC views co "as high quality" and constructive on long-term fundamentals, but adds, "the current risk/reward has become less compelling at these levels"

** CIBC says PT raised on "growing upside potential related to defense"

** "The company continues to benefit from a multi-year runway for growth in its higher-margin defense and aftermarket businesses," BMO says

** Following are the new ratings and PTs:

data.title: Italy's Hera targets core profit of 1.76 billion euros by 2029
data.description:
Italian regional utility Hera targets core profit growth to 1.76 billion euros ($2.06 billion) by 2029 under its new industrial plan, it said on Wednesday.

The Bologna-based company said its 2025 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation would exceed 1.53 billion euros, compared with analysts' 1.56-billion-euro estimate in a company-provided consensus. It also flagged a preliminary net profit of more than 460 million euros.

Its shares fell as much as 3% after it announced the industrial plan and preliminary earnings.

Hera is planning to invest 5.5 billion euros under the new plan, up by around 40% compared to the last five years, Executive Chairman Cristian Fabbri said in a statement. These investments will support sustainable industrial development and increased resilience of Hera's infrastructures, also through innovation, he added.

The company will allocate about 100 million euros for mergers and acquisitions over the next five years, matching its previous M&A dealings and underscoring its strategy to pursue external growth.

Hera also aims to raise its dividend by 27% to 19 euro cents per share by 2029. For 2025, it plans to distribute 16 euro cents per share.

On Monday, Hera pledged to buy Sostelia to boost its water treatment operations in a deal worth 138 million euros.

($1 = 0.8534 euros)

data.title: EU Lawmakers Ask for Legal Opinion on South America Trade Deal
data.description:
By Edith Hancock

European Union lawmakers voted to assess the legality of the EU's landmark trade agreement with South American countries signed on Saturday.

Members of the European Parliament on Wednesday narrowly voted to send the bloc's deal with Mercosur countries Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay to the Court of Justice of the EU for a judicial review on Wednesday. The motion passed with 334 votes in favour and 324 against, with 11 abstentions.

The move delays MEPs' ability to vote on the deal itself. A legal opinion from judges in Luxembourg can take around two years.

The vote is a setback for the commission, which has negotiated the Mercosur pact on and off for roughly 25 years. The commission has also used the deal to advertise the EU as a reliable trading partner in the wake of U.S. President Trump's tariffs.

The European Commission — the bloc's executive arm overseeing global trade policy — does have the option to provisionally implement the agreement while legal procedures are ongoing.

A commission spokesperson said on Wednesday the EU executive will continue to engage with members of the European Parliament.

"The commission strongly regrets the decision," spokesperson Olof Gill said. "This comes at a time when EU producers and exporters urgently need access to new markets, and when the EU must deliver on its diversification agenda and demonstrate that it remains a predictable and reliable partner," he said.

Write to Edith Hancock at edith.hancock@wsj.com

data.title: Veidekke: To Renew Railway Tracks for Bane NOR
data.description:
Today Veidekke has signed a contract with Bane NOR to carry out track renewalonfive sections across three regions in Eastern Norway. The contract is aconstruction contract valued at NOK 460 million excluding VAT, plus options.

"We are very pleased to have won this competition and appreciate the trustBaneNOR has placed in us. This will be extensive and intensive work where we willdraw on our broad expertise and experience across all railway technicaldisciplines," says Magnus S. Kristensen, Manager of Veidekke Bane.

The track renewal works will take place on segments of the Røros Line, theDovreLine, the Gardermoen Line (Romeriksporten tunnel), the Upper Main Line and theGjøvik Line. In total, the contract includes the replacement of 46 kilometresoftracks, the addition of approximately 90,000 tonnes of ballast, and thereplacement of more than 75,000 railway sleepers.

"Track renewal is essential to maintain safety and punctuality on the railway.This contract will help ensure more stable and reliable transport for bothpassengers and freight. We look forward to good cooperation with Veidekke onthis important assignment. Veidekke has the competence and experience thatgivesus confidence that this extensive project will be carried out efficiently andwith high quality," says Henning Bråtebæk, Executive Vice President forOperations and Maintenance at Bane NOR.

Veidekke Bane was established in 1999 and performs renewal and maintenance ofrailways and tramways. The company operates nationwide, has an annual turnoverof around NOK 600 million and employs 180 people with high-level expertiseacross all railway technical disciplines.

The planned construction period for the main part of the works is from earlyMayto mid-September. The contract will be included in Veidekke's order backlogforthe first quarter of 2026.

For further information, please contact:

Manager Magnus Sutherland Kristensen, Veidekke Bane, tel +47 92 22 90 81,magnus.kristensen@veidekke.no

Communications Manager Helge Dieset, Veidekke, tel +47 90 55 33 22,helge.dieset@veidekke.no

Veidekke's press photos (https://veidekke.fotoware.cloud/fotoweb/archives/5028-Pressebilder-Veidekke/)Subscribe to notices fromVeidekke (http://veidekke.com/no/borsmeldinger/abonner-pa-meldinger/)Veidekke (http://www.veidekke.com/) is one of Scandinavia's largestcontractors.In addition to undertaking all types of building and civil engineeringassignments, the group also maintains roads and produces asphalt andaggregates.Veidekke emphasises stakeholder involvement and local experience. Its annualturnover is approximately NOK 41 billion, and half of its 8,000 employees ownshares in the company. Veidekke is listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange and hasposted a profit every year since its inception in 1936.

data.title: United's perks and loyalty strategy pays off, as its stock surges after earnings
data.description:
By Claudia Assis

United Airlines beats expectations for 14th straight quarter, and outlook for 2026 comes in ahead of forecast

A United Airlines jet departing Vancouver's international airport. United reported quarterly earnings late Tuesday.

United Airlines late Tuesday reported better-than-expected profits for the holiday quarter, saying its focus on loyal fliers is paying off and that "strong momentum" continued into the new year.

The airline's 2026 guidance also came in ahead of expectations at the top end. United Chief Executive Scott Kirby said that the results "are built on winning more and more brand-loyal customers." Shares of United (UAL) gained 3.6% in the extended session.

It was United's 14th straight quarterly beat, according to FactSet; it recorded its last miss in July 2022, when it reported that year's April-June quarter.

United and rival Delta Air Lines have waged an arms race of sorts for travelers willing to pay more for creature comforts and perks such as priority boarding.

Both are offering more nonstop and international destinations, with greater emphasis on on-time departures, lounges and tiered service to lure wealthier travelers.

United called for 2026 adjusted per-share earnings between $12 and $14, ahead of the FactSet consensus for $13.19.

The better-than-expected results came despite the U.S. government shutdown last fall, which slowed U.S. airlines' momentum in a crucial time of the year.

The shutdown was "an opportunity to do the right thing by our customers and further build their trust," United said, highlighting that it offered full refunds to travelers even if their flights weren't canceled. The company said it incurred about $250 million in fourth-quarter charges related to the shutdown, including impact on bookings.

Revenue from its premium fares was up 9% for the fourth quarter and 11% for the full year, while loyalty revenue was up 10% for the fourth quarter and 9% for the full year, United said. Revenue from basic-economy fares grew slower, or 7% for the fourth quarter and 5% for the full year.

"Strong revenue momentum has continued into 2026," the company said, adding that the week to Jan. 4 was the highest in terms of flown revenue in its history, and the week ending on Jan. 11 was the highest ticketing week and the highest week for business sales ever.

United reported adjusted revenue of $3.10 a share, ahead of Wall Street expectations for $2.93 a share, according to FactSet. Revenue rose 5% to $15.4 billion, meeting FactSet consensus. United reported adjusted EPS of $3.26 on revenue of $14.7 billion for the fourth quarter of 2024.

United's optimistic tone corresponded with what some on Wall Street are seeing, including detecting an uptick in business air travel, which helps United and other U.S. major airlines.

Corporate air travel ended 2025 "depressed," analysts at Citi said in a recent note, but there has been a "meaningful" bounce for it at the start of 2026, which is favoring the "supermajors" such as Delta Air Lines (DAL) and United.

Delta reported fourth-quarter earnings last week and offered investors a disappointing outlook.

-Claudia Assis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

(i)         Writing, selling, purchasing or varying

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description e.g. call option

  • Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.

  • Number of securities to which option relates

  • Exercise price per unit

  • Type

    e.g. American, European etc.

  • Expiry date

  • Option money paid/ received per unit

(ii)        Exercise

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

    e.g. call option

  • Exercising/ exercised against

  • Number of securities

  • Exercise price per unit

(d)        Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)

  • Class of relevant security

  • Nature of dealing

    e.g. subscription, conversion

  • Details

  • Price per unit (if applicable)

4.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(c)        Attachments

  • Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?

  • NO

Date of disclosure:

21 January 2026

Contact name:

Richard Howard

Telephone number:

+44 (0)207 568 9128

Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service and must also be emailed to the Takeover Panel at monitoring@thetakeoverpanel.org.uk

The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's dealing disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.

Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our Privacy Policy.  END  FEOSEAFDFEMSESF

data.title: Political and General News Events from January 21
data.description:
For other diaries, please see:

Political and General News

Top Economic Events

Emerging Markets Economic Events

Government Debt Auctions

U.S. Federal Reserve

Today in Washington

----------------------------------------------------------------

This diary is filed daily. ** Indicates new events

---------------------------------------------------------------

WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 21

** NAIROBI - The German Foreign Affairs Minister Dr. Johann Wadephul is set to make a two-day official visit to Kenya as part of strengthening collaboration between the two countries.(To Jan. 22)

** WARSAW - Poland's Defence Minister Wladyslaw Kosiniak-Kamysz will host Latvia's Defence Minister Andris Spruds in Warsaw for bilateral talks on security cooperation and regional defence issues. - 1100 GMT

** ANKARA - Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan will host his South Korean counterpart Cho Hyun in Ankara.

** BRUSSELS - European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and European Council President Antonio Costa address European Parliament on topic of geopolitical tensions and the need for the EU to be stronger and more sovereign - 0800 GMT

NEW DELHI - Spain's Minister of Foreign Affairs, European Union and Cooperation, Jose Manuel Albares will visit India (final day)

TARAWA/NGERULMUD - New Zealand Foreign Minister Winston Peters will visit Kiribati and Palau as New Zealand continues to deepen and build on its relationships across the Pacific (To Jan. 22)

DAVOS, Switzerland - Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif will leave for a four-day visit to Switzerland (To Jan. 23)

BAKU - NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Šekerinska will visit Azerbaijan (To Jan. 22)

DAVOS, Switzerland - U.S. President Donald Trump arrives in Davos for the World Economic Forum.

DAVOS, Switzerland - Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi delivers a special address at the World Economic Forum. - 0830 GMT

DAVOS, Switzerland - WEF panel titled "Can India Become the Third Largest Economy in the World?" with speakers including India's Minister of Electronics and Information Technology Ashwini Vaishnaw, former IMF First Deputy Managing Director Gita Gopinath and Bharti Enterprises Chairman Sunil Bharti Mittal - 0915 GMT

STRASBOURG, France - Portugal's President Marcelo REbelo de Sousa addresses EU Parliament in Strasbourg - 1130 GMT

DAVOS, Switzerland - US President Donald Trump takes part in meetings with foreign leaders and in a reception with business leaders. - 1445 GMT

DAVOS, Switzerland - Polish President Karol Nawrocki and NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte participate in a WEF panel titled "Can Europe Defend Itself?" alongside Finnish President Alexander Stubb, European Investment Bank President Nadia Calvino and Sanofi CEO Paul Hudson. - 0915 GMT.

MOSCOW - Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas will visit Moscow (To Jan. 22)

BRUSSELS - A meeting of NATO chiefs of defence takes place at alliance headquarters in Brussels. (To Jan. 22)

DAVOS, Switzerland - Singapore President Tharman Shanmugaratnam will make a working visit to Davos, Switzerland for the World Economic Forum (WEF) Annual Meeting, where he will meet government leaders from various countries, and business, finance, and philanthropic leaders. (To Jan. 22)

- - - - - - - - -

THURSDAY, JANUARY 22

** NEW YORK - South Korea's Prime Minister Kim Min-seok will meet with top US officials to discuss pending issues in Washington and New York on a five-day trip to the United States (To Jan. 26)

** DAVOS, Switzerland - Kazakhstan's President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev will travel to Davos and participate in the signing ceremony of U.S. President Donald Trump's "Board of Peace" charter

** DAVOS, Switzerland - Polish President Karol Nawrocki speaks to Polish media at the World Economic Forum in Davos. - 0930 GMT

** BRUSSELS - European Council President Antonio Costa is convening a special summit of leaders from the bloc's 27 member countries to discuss transatlantic relations following U.S. President Donald Trump's threats to impose new tariffs on goods from a list of EU countries over his demand to acquire Greenland. - 1600 GMT

STOCKHOLM - Denmark's Foreign Minister Lars Lokke Rasmussen visits Sweden to talk Greenland and NATO's role in Arctic security.

DAVOS, Switzerland - U.S. President Donald Trump takes part in "Board of Peace" charter announcement and departs Davos. - 0930 GMT

DAVOS, Switzerland - World Trade Organization Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala and South Korean Minister of Trade Yeo Han-Koo attend a panel at the World Economic Forum. - 1400 GMT

DAVOS, Switzerland - Davos panel discussion on the Mideast economy. Speakers include Ali Ahmed Al Kuwari, Minister of Finance of the State of Qatar; Khalifa Abdullah Al-Ajeel AI-Askar, Minister of Commerce and Industry of Kuwait; Hisham Ezz-Al-Arab, Chief Executive Officer, Commercial International Bank, Egypt; Ahmed Kouchouk, Minister of Finance of Egypt. - 1515 GMT

DAVOS, Switzerland - Davos discussion with IMF's Georgieva on growth. Speakers include Kristalina Georgieva, Managing Director, IMF. - 1515 GMT

- - - - - - - - -

FRIDAY, JANUARY 23

DAVOS, Switzerland - Davos panel discussion on global economic outlook. Speakers include Mohammed Al-Jadaan, Minister of Finance of Saudi Arabia; Kristalina Georgieva, Managing Director, International Monetary Fund; Christine Lagarde, President of the European Central Bank. - 1000 GMT.

GENEVA, SWITZERLAND - Press briefing in Geneva with Iran's Foreign Affairs Minister Abbas Araghtchi. - 0730 GMT

- - - - - - - - -

SATURDAY, JANUARY 24

LOME, Togo – World Day of the African Culture

- - - - - - - - -

SUNDAY, JANUARY 25

NEW DELHI - The President of the European Council, Antonio Luis Santos da Costa, and the President of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, will be visiting India. Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has invited the EU leaders to attend the 77th Republic Day celebrations as chief guests. (To Jan. 27)

EGYPT – 15th anniversary of beginning of the Egyptian revolution, a series of mass popular protests leading to the resignation of President Hosni Mubarak.

MYANMAR - Myanmar to hold third phase of election voting

- - - - - - - - -

MONDAY, JANUARY 26

** HAMBURG, Germany - German Chancellor Friedrich Merz hosts the third international North Sea Summit in Hamburg, with several heads of state and government plus their energy ministers attending.

BRUSSELS – EU General Affairs Council.

- - - - - - - - -

TUESDAY, JANUARY 27

BERLIN, Germany - German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius speaks at a conference of the Association of German Chambers of Industry and Commerce (DIHK) in Berlin - 0900 GMT

- - - - - - - - -

WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 28

VILNIUS - King Frederik X and Queen Mary of Denmark will pay a state visit to Lithuania (to Jan. 29).

- - - - - - - - -

THURSDAY, JANUARY 29

** MACTAN, Philippines - ASEAN Foreign Ministers hold a retreat in central Philippines to exchange views on regional and international issues and set the bloc's agenda for the year.

ATHENS - Greece hosts energy summit in Athens.

BRUSSELS – EU Foreign Affairs Council.

- - - - - - - - -

data.title: Serve Robotics Signs Agreement and Plan of Merger With Diligent Robotics
data.description:
Serve Robotics entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger to acquire Diligent Robotics. The all-stock deal values the consideration at $29.0 million, subject to net debt and other adjustments, including a potential $5.3 million earnout tied to milestones. The number of Serve shares to be issued will be based on a $14.3794 per-share 10-day VWAP. Upon closing, Diligent will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Serve Robotics.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Agreement and Plan of Merger
  • Counterparty: Diligent Robotics
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 19 2026 / same
  • Reason: Expand healthcare robotics capabilities and scale operations

Original SEC Filing: Serve Robotics Inc. /DE/ [ SERV ] - 8-K - Jan. 21, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: DANA Inc Announces Strong Preliminary 2025 Financial Results and 2026 Outlook
data.description:
On January 21, 2026, DANA Inc reported strong preliminary 2025 financial results and provided an optimistic 2026 outlook.

Key Highlights:

  • 2025 sales reached approximately $7.5 billion.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was about $600 million, 8% of sales.
  • Completed Off-Highway business sale valued at $2.7 billion.
  • Returned $704 million to shareholders, repurchasing 34 million shares.
  • 2026 margin guidance raised to a midpoint of 10.5%.

Original SEC Filing: DANA Inc [ DAN ] - 8-K - Jan. 21, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
